creator: exploringlondon
caption:
This is London’s oldest restaurant, established in 1798! Spanning the reign of 10 monarchs, it’s only been owned by 4 different families over this time and it really feels like you’ve stepped back in time. The walls are covered with paintings and photos, newspaper articles, antlers and more, making it feel like a museum and it’s such an experience dining amongst it all.

The menu is traditional British and they do have a vegetarian menu if you request it (although limited). Definitely worth visiting when you’re in London ❤️

📍 @rules_restaurant @coventgardenldn, 35 Maiden Ln, London WC2E 7LB

#rulesrestaurant #londonsoldestrestaurant

transcription.text: The next time you're in Covent Garden, look out for this restaurant. It's called Rules and it's also known as London's oldest restaurant established in 1798. Head inside and it feels like you've walked into a museum. There is so much history on the walls and it gives the restaurant so much character. And if you've watched Downton Abbey, you may also recognise this place as a filming location. The menu consists of traditional British food and when it first opened, it was specialising in oysters. And you'll still find oysters on the menu today. And they do also have a vegetarian menu. This restaurant has seen so much history spanning the reign of 10 monarchs, but has only been owned by four families. Definitely a bucket list place to visit in London.
